source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
For the power system shown in the <image>, the specifications of the components are the following:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \text{G1: } 25 \text{ kV}, 100 \text{ MVA}, X = 9\% \\
& \text{G2: } 25 \text{ kV}, 100 \text{ MVA}, X = 9\% \\
& \text{T1: } 25 \text{ kV} / 220 \text{ kV}, 90 \text{ MVA}, X = 12\% \\
& \text{T2: } 220 \text{ kV} / 25 \text{ kV}, 90 \text{ MVA}, X = 12\%
\end{aligned}
$$
Line 1: 200 kV, X = 150 ohms
Choose 25 kV as the base voltage at the generator G1, and 200 MVA as the MVA base. The impedance diagram is:
extraction_notes: image field is a PIL.Image object, not an extractable path or URL
answer letter mapped to option text using options embedded in the problem text

issues: metadata notes that the original image field was a PIL.Image object, not an extractable path or URL
metadata.image_paths is empty while top-level image_paths contains 1 path

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'shown in the image'
the choices are diagram labels (A/B/C/D), so selecting the answer depends on visual comparison with diagram options
multi_step: question requires forming an impedance diagram on a new common base (25 kV at G1, 200 MVA base)
multiple component specifications must be combined: G1, G2, T1, T2, and Line 1
the line reactance is given in ohms while machine/transformer reactances are given in percent, implying at least one unit/base conversion step plus diagram matching
final answer is not a direct lookup; it requires deriving equivalent per-unit impedances and then choosing among 4 diagram options
verifiability: answer_type is short_text with a discrete multiple-choice target ('diagram B')
choices are explicitly enumerated as A/B/C/D, so final-answer checking is straightforward
the underlying calculations (base conversion and impedance normalization) are structured and can be checked step by step

decision_reasons: question text, answer, and 1 associated image are all present, so the core semantics appear recoverable
the sample has high initial scoring on image dependency, multi-step reasoning, and verifiability
